title: Hieracium ovaliceps
taxonomy: Hieracium ovaliceps belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium ovaliceps is classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which includes approximately 1000 species of plants. The genus Hieracium is further divided into several subgenera, with Hieracium ovaliceps likely falling within one of these subgroups. The species Hieracium ovaliceps is a type of hawkweed, a name that is commonly associated with the genus Hieracium. Further classification of Hieracium ovaliceps may involve the identification of specific subgenus, species subgroup, or even cultivar designations.

intro: Hieracium ovaliceps, also known as Oval-leaved Hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium ovaliceps include flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium ovaliceps is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.
